domain_name: Bias in measurement of outcomes
sq_text: Could the outcome measure have been influenced by knowledge of the intervention received?
quote: SI and suicide attempt were identified by ICD-10 diagnosis codes R45.851 and T14.91, respectively.
sq_note: Code-based ascertainment is largely objective, although clinical recognition and coding could potentially be influenced by treatment-related monitoring.
domain_rationale: The same objective coding approach was applied to both groups, but the authors acknowledge likely low sensitivity and substantial underreporting of suicide-related outcomes. Given the very small number of detected events, this misclassification could materially obscure the neuropsychiatric effect even without demonstrated differential ascertainment.
judge_reason: Resolved by reconciliation round (both reviewers converged): The colleague grounds a point I underweighted: the authors' acknowledged low sensitivity and likely undercounting, combined with the very small number of detected suicide-related events, means the misclassification could materially obscure the true effect rather than being a manageable non-differential nuisance. Treating the non-differential coding as reassuring was the absence-of-evidence fallacy the reconciliation warns against, so the more cautious Serious rating is warranted.

judge_reason: Resolved by reconciliation round (both reviewers converged): The colleague grounds a point I underweighted: the outcomes are unvalidated healthcare-use proxies whose measurement can differ systematically by group (help-seeking, documentation, practice patterns), and the authors explicitly could not control for variation in practice patterns or utilization across organizations. domain_name: Bias due to missing outcome data
sq_text: Were data for this outcome available for all, or nearly all, participants randomized?
quote: A total of 1789 patients (71.9%) in the liraglutide group, as compared with 801 patients (64.4%) in the placebo group, completed 56 weeks of treatment
sq_note: Substantial and differential non-completion (28% vs 36%) means outcome data were not available for nearly all randomized participants.
domain_rationale: Attrition was substantial and differential between arms, and the text provides no analysis showing the neuropsychiatric result is unbiased by missing data, though missingness does not clearly depend on true psychiatric outcome.
judge_reason: Resolved by reconciliation round (both reviewers converged): The colleague's citation that adverse-event ascertainment was tied to the treatment period (onset no later than 14 days after last dose) combined with differential early withdrawal—which I had noted but underweighted—establishes a concrete pathway by which neuropsychiatric events in the more-attriting liraglutide arm could be systematically missed in a way dependent on their true occurrence. No sensitivity analysis addresses this, so differential missingness with plausible outcome-dependence warrants High rather than Some concerns.

domain_name: Bias in selection of participants into the study
sq_text: Was selection of participants into the study (or into the analysis) based on participant characteristics observed after the start of intervention?

domain_name: Bias due to deviations from intended interventions

domain_rationale: Adherence was not adequately observed or controlled, and potentially important psychiatric and other co-interventions were not shown to be balanced. The analysis did not appropriately estimate an effect of starting and adhering to treatment under these conditions.
judge_reason: Resolved by reconciliation round (both reviewers converged): The colleague grounded 4.4 in the actual cohort definition (treated = ≥24 months of achieved GLP-1RA exposure after starting therapy), which I had marked as no-evidence; combined with uncontrolled adherence this defines exposure by post-baseline persistence rather than an intention-to-adhere contrast, producing expected rather than merely uncertain bias. With psychiatric co-interventions not shown balanced for a neuropsychiatric outcome, the more cautious Serious rating is warranted.

domain_name: Bias due to confounding
sq_text: Is there potential for confounding of the effect of intervention in this study?
quote: Residual confounding, particularly by indication, might occur, as in all observational studies, despite the within-individual comparisons and adjustment for the temporal order of exposures and concomitant medication use, covering both the severity of diabetes and the psychiatric condition.
sq_note: Authors explicitly acknowledge confounding potential in this observational design.
domain_rationale: The within-individual design controls all time-invariant confounding and adjusts for key comedications, but time-varying confounding by indication—disease severity, glycaemic control, and weight change, all plausibly linked to both GLP-1 initiation/discontinuation and mental-illness worsening—is unmeasured and acknowledged by the authors as residual confounding. This is the dominant, plausible source of material bias.

domain_name: Bias due to confounding

domain_rationale: The within-individual design controls all time-invariant confounding and adjusts for key comedications, but time-varying confounding by indication—disease severity, glycaemic control, and weight change, all plausibly linked to both GLP-1 initiation/discontinuation and mental-illness worsening—is unmeasured and acknowledged by the authors as residual confounding. domain_rationale: The primary outcome and three principal designs were described, but no protocol or analysis plan is provided and several analyses are explicitly post hoc. Some risk of selective analytical reporting therefore remains.

domain_name: Bias in selection of participants into the study
sq_text: Was selection of participants into the study (or into the analysis) based on participant characteristics observed after the start of intervention?
quote: To identify incident users, patients with any prior record of tirze￾patide exposure before the index date were excluded, consistent with a new-user design.
sq_note: A new-user incident design was used, minimizing selection on post-intervention characteristics.
domain_rationale: The new-user design and exclusion of patients who died within 30 days reduce immortal-time bias, but the asymmetric index-date definition (drug initiation vs diagnosis date) introduces potential misalignment of time zero between groups that is only partially addressed by sensitivity analysis.
judge_reason: Resolved by reconciliation round (both reviewers converged): The colleague cites text I overlooked: deaths within 30 days were excluded in both arms and outcome follow-up started at 30 days, so participants were selected on the basis of surviving a post-index-date window — a post-intervention selection that can bias the effect, especially since all-cause mortality is part of the composite outcome. Combined with the misaligned comparator time zero (diagnosis rather than initiation of an active alternative), this raises the domain to Serious.
